parsed_review.summary: Read-only independent review of CP00-181 (RP05 Matter Core descriptor-only API/interface + early UI state bridge, risk_class C, 150 units) via Read/Grep/Glob only. The pack is honestly pre-closeout: production_ready=false with all gates pending across manifest, construction-inspection, command-evidence, and claude-review-result, so there is no unsupported production-ready or enterprise-trust claim to refute. Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries verified (all no-write attestation flags false; descriptor_only:true, runtime_dispatch_enabled:false; no fs/network/db/graph I/O; serialized result omits raw payload/descriptor body; synthetic_only:true, no_real_client_data:true). Claude is confirmed read-only reviewer and NOT final approver, with enterprise trust not claimed from local validation; Hermes patterns are review-packet/receipt only and not embedded in product runtime. Deliverable/phase/micro distributions all reconcile to 150 and match the validators; the manifest plan_binding_snapshot fallback is sound. Review-evidence hardening passes: readiness auth preflight returned non-empty valid JSON (not auth-fail/0-byte/malformed/fenced+prose/tool-call-shaped); raw-output and receipt are correctly absent. Unrelated dirty/untracked files were not inferred into pack scope. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with a single non-blocking P3 (descriptor_ref id-precedence) and no P0/P1/P2; this review is one input to the C05 gate and is not final approval.

findings.title: descriptor_ref precedence resolves to matter_id for all non-Matter model types, making the other id branches unreachable and descriptor_ref non-unique per entity
findings.details: descriptorRefFor (service.js:235-249) builds primaryId via the null-coalescing chain descriptor.matter_id ?? member_id ?? task_id ?? section_id ?? edge_id ?? node_id ?? wiki_id. Because matter_id is a required field on every non-Matter Matter Core type (member, task, section, edge, node, wiki), descriptor.matter_id is always populated and short-circuits the chain, so the subsequent branches are dead code. The resulting descriptor_ref is `${model_type}:${matter_id}` rather than `${model_type}:${entity_own_id}`, so two entities of the same model_type under the same matter (e.g. two MatterMember rows) collide on an identical descriptor_ref instead of being distinguished by their own primary id. This is benign in the current pack because descriptor_only:true and runtime_dispatch_enabled:false (service.js:371-372): descriptor_ref is a never-write reference/display hint and is not used for any persistence, lookup, permission, audit, or dispatch path, and the serialized API result omits the descriptor body. No correctness, security, or no-write-boundary impact under the current descriptor-only architecture.
findings.recommendation: Before any future pack enables runtime dispatch or uses descriptor_ref for entity identity/lookup, reorder the precedence so each model_type resolves to its own primary id (e.g. select by model_type, or place the entity-specific id ahead of matter_id), or remove the unreachable branches to make the matter-scoped intent explicit. No change required for CP00-181 closeout.
